join_order.notes: Use the dense adsh lookup to discard non-2023 filings during the sequential num scan.
Empirically best join order from sampling_join_order.cpp: partitioned num-hash then sequential pre scan beat pre-then-num-index on the full dataset.

data_structures.fact_join: radix_partitioned compact_hash_map on filtered num keyed by (adsh,tag,version) storing {sum,count}; partition count chosen so each partition hash table fits in LLC before probing sequential pre rows
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map on (name_id, stmt_id, tag_id, plabel_id), allocated per partition and merged once

optimization_notes: Root cause: the remaining hot time comes from cache-miss-heavy random probes into the 157 MB num sorted index and double binary-search work for roughly 550k filtered pre keys, while the filtered num side can be consumed sequentially. Strategy category B with plan restructuring: filter sub first, scan num sequentially with uom zone-map pruning and dense adsh lookup, aggregate surviving rows by (adsh,tag,version) into LLC-sized radix partitions, then scan pre sequentially with stmt zone-map pruning and probe those partitions to expand matching plabel rows into the final group-by.
